title: Mildly Yeasty Thai Rice Khanom Thai
description: A crunchy and yeasty Thai street food featuring lime juice and shio-cure, perfect for a quick snack. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly dish is cooked to perfection in a copper pot.
instructions: In a large copper pot, combine the Thai rice flour, coconut milk, lime juice, shio-cure, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Whisk the mixture until it is smooth and free of lumps.
Cover the pot with a lid and let the mixture rest in a warm place for 30 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the rested mixture into the hot oil.
Fry the khanom thai until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the khanom thai from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the khanom thai warm and enjoy.
